data.title: Coinbase estimates up to $400M in customer payouts after data breach
data.description:
Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) expects to pay $180M-$400M in reimbursements and remediation after a recent data breach exposed sensitive customer information and led to users being tricked into sending funds to the attackers, it said on Thursday.

On May 11, the crypto exchange "received an email communication from an unknown threat actor claiming to have obtained information about certain Coinbase customer accounts, as well as internal Coinbase documentation, including materials relating to customer-service and account-management systems," it disclosed in an SEC filing.

The attackers allegedly paid overseas support staff to gain internal access and gather customer data. Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) noted it has already flagged and fired those involved.

From there, the cyber criminals demanded $20M to keep the stolen information private, though Coinbase (COIN) rejected the extortion attempt and instead put up a $20M reward for information leading to their arrest and conviction, according to a blog post.

In addition to the reward fund, the company has tagged the attackers' addresses so that the authorities can trace the stolen funds and work to recover stolen assets.

The scammers did not access login credentials, private keys, any ability to move or access customer funds, access to Coinbase Prime accounts, and access to any Coinbase or Coinbase customer hot or cold wallets.

COIN shares gapped down 7.6% in late morning trading.

data.title: Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Automotive Properties Real Estate Investment Trust press release (OTC:APPTF): Q1 AFFO per Unit of $0.247 (diluted) and paid regular cash distributions of $0.201 per Unit.
  • Rental revenue in Q1 2025 increased by 2.1% to $23.9 million, compared to $23.4 million in Q1 2024. 
  • Net income and other comprehensive income of $7.6 million in Q1 2025, compared to $20.9 million in Q1 2024. 
  • FFO in Q1 2025 increased 4.1% to $12.6 million, or $0.251 per Unit.
  • AFFO in Q1 2025 increased 6.0% to $12.4 million, or $0.247 per Unit.

data.title: Investor uncertainty grows as market rallies: Bear trap or Bull run?
data.description:
Investor sentiment appears increasingly divided as questions loom over the future direction of Wall Street. While markets initially stumbled following "Liberation Day," they have since staged a notable rebound. Still, a growing number of market participants are left wondering: is this simply a bear market rally, or the beginning of a sustained climb?

For those caught between the fear of missing out on potential gains and the desire for downside protection, Seeking Alpha has compiled a list of stocks that may offer a balance of both safety and growth. 

Outlined below is a curated list of companies that can cover both the idea of growth and safety in the form of dividends. The criteria below covers U.S. stocks with market capitalizations of $100B or greater along with divided yields of at least 3% and Seeking Alpha quant grades of a B- or higher as it pertains to growth. List is ranked by year-to-date performance. 

No. 10: Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) -7.3% YTD.

No. 9: American Express (NYSE:AXP) +1.1% YTD.

No. 8: Prologis (NYSE:PLD) +1.8% YTD.

No. 7: S&P Global (NYSE:SPGI) +2.8% YTD.

No. 6: Booking Holdings (NASDAQ:BKNG) +4.9% YTD.

No. 5: Gilead Sciences (NASDAQ:GILD) +6.5% YTD.

No. 4: T-Mobile US (NASDAQ:TMUS) +6.6% YTD.

No. 3: Goldman Sachs (NYSE:GS) +6.8% YTD.

No. 2: Mastercard Corporation (NYSE:MA) +8.8% YTD.

No. 1: Progressive Corporation (NYSE:PGR) +16.1% YTD. 

For reference, Seeking Alpha's Quant system is driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics. 

For further exposure towards dividends and growth, investors can turn towards exchange traded funds for further diversification.

Dividend ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VIG), (NYSEARCA:SCHD), (NYSEARCA:VYM), (NYSEARCA:DGRO), (NYSEARCA:SDY), (NASDAQ:DVY), (NASDAQ:DGRW), (BATS:NOBL), (NYSEARCA:HDV), (NYSEARCA:FVD), (NASDAQ:RDVY), (NASDAQ:VIGI), and (NASDAQ:SDVY).

Growth ETFs: (NYSEARCA:VUG), (NYSEARCA:IWF), (NYSEARCA:IVW), (NYSEARCA:SCHG), (NYSEARCA:SPYG), (NASDAQ:VONG), (NYSEARCA:MGK), (NASDAQ:IUSG), and (NYSEARCA:VBK).
